name: Mercury
subtitle: The Swift Planet
description: Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system and closest to the Sun. It has a heavily cratered surface resembling our Moon. Despite being nearest to the Sun, it is not the hottest planet — that title belongs to Venus. Mercury has virtually no atmosphere to retain heat, causing extreme temperature swings between day and night.

name: Venus
subtitle: The Evening Star
description: Venus is the second planet from the Sun and the hottest planet in our solar system. Its thick, toxic atmosphere traps heat in a runaway greenhouse effect. Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most planets and is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to their similar size and mass.

name: Earth
subtitle: The Blue Planet
description: Earth is the third planet from the Sun and the only known world to harbor life. About 71% of its surface is covered in water. Its atmosphere protects life from harmful solar radiation. Earth's magnetic field shields the planet from the solar wind.

name: Mars
subtitle: The Red Planet
description: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun with a distinctive reddish appearance due to iron oxide on its surface. It has the tallest mountain (Olympus Mons) and the longest canyon (Valles Marineris) in the solar system. Mars has been a prime target for exploration in the search for past microbial life.

name: Jupiter
subtitle: The Giant Planet
description: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system, a gas giant with a mass more than twice that of all other planets combined. Its Great Red Spot is a storm larger than Earth that has been raging for centuries. Jupiter has a faint ring system and at least 95 known moons.

name: Saturn
subtitle: The Ringed Planet
description: Saturn is the sixth planet from the Sun, best known for its spectacular ring system made of ice and rock particles. It is the second-largest planet and has the lowest density of any planet — it would float in water. Saturn's moon Titan has a thick atmosphere and liquid methane lakes.

name: Uranus
subtitle: The Ice Giant
description: Uranus is the seventh planet from the Sun, an ice giant with a blue-green color from methane in its atmosphere. It rotates on its side with an axial tilt of 98 degrees, likely caused by a massive ancient collision. Uranus has faint rings and a fleet of 27 known moons.

name: Neptune
subtitle: The Windy Planet
description: Neptune is the eighth and farthest planet from the Sun, a dark, cold ice giant with the strongest winds in the solar system reaching up to 2,100 km/h. Its vivid blue color comes from methane in its atmosphere. Neptune was the first planet located through mathematical prediction rather than observation.
